epics.goal: Load approved work-order sources into bronze with lineage, rejection handling, and local E2E proof.
epics.name: SAP work-order ingestion foundation
epics.sequencing_rationale: Start with one source-to-bronze vertical slice before normalization and cross-source reconciliation.

acceptance_criteria: Valid rows are represented in bronze with source_file_id and source_row_number.
Invalid rows are rejected with reason code and source lineage.
The focused parser verification command passes.
tasks.behavior: Parse an approved SAP work-order CSV and persist one bronze record per accepted raw row with source file and row identifiers
negative_cases: Malformed rows are not silently dropped or accepted.
tasks.objective: Load one SAP work-order source file into bronze with row-level lineage

technical_spec_refs.reason: Task must use only approved dependency choices.
Task implementation must follow approved module boundaries.
Task must preserve lineage/idempotency and avoid cloud mutation.
Task verification must follow approved testing implications.

cloud_e2e.not_required_reason: Cloud Azure/Databricks execution is outside build-auto and requires goal-auto or explicit environment authority.

coverage_map.criterion: Valid rows are represented in bronze with source_file_id and source_row_number.
Invalid rows are rejected with reason code and source lineage.
The focused parser verification command passes.

evidence_required: local integration test output proving DB/filesystem boundary behaviour
what_to_test: fixture CSV is read from local filesystem
accepted rows persist to bronze store
rejected rows persist with reason code and source lineage

evidence_required: local E2E command output and assertions for counts/lineage
what_to_test: run ingestion against fixture landing directory
verify source lineage is queryable end to end

test_expectations.tier_rationale: This task introduces a source-to-bronze pipeline slice with persistence, lineage, rejection handling, and fixture-based execution.

evidence_required: focused RED/GREEN unit tests under tests/unit or equivalent
